seo.metaDescription: Build hotel search, booking, payment, webhook, and reporting workflows with Wink developer access and documentation.
seo.ogTitle: Wink Developers | Build Hotel Commerce into Your Product
seo.ogDescription: Connect hotel search, booking, payment, webhook, and reporting workflows into apps, loyalty products, marketplaces, agencies, and partner platforms.

fields.heading: Build hotel commerce into your product.
fields.body: Build hotel search, booking, payment, webhook, and reporting workflows with Wink developer access and documentation.

fields.heading: Choose the next step that fits your role.
fields.body: Build hotel search, booking, payment, webhook, and reporting workflows with Wink developer access and documentation.

cards.title: Search
cards.body: Hotel discovery, availability, rate context, location logic, and eligible supply.

cards.title: Booking
cards.body: Room selection, package logic, add-ons, guest details, confirmation, and post-booking routing.

cards.title: Payment
cards.body: Stripe-powered payment flow and booking-linked settlement context.

cards.title: Webhooks
cards.body: Booking events, status changes, partner attribution, and reporting triggers.

cards.title: SDKs
cards.body: Developer-friendly implementation paths for selected languages and partner use cases.

cards.title: Reporting
cards.body: Confirmed booking, campaign, partner, and channel performance data where approved.

cards.title: Travel apps
cards.body: Add bookable hotel supply inside destination, itinerary, or recommendation flows.

cards.title: Banks and loyalty programs
cards.body: Connect hotel booking paths to member benefits, rewards, or travel redemptions.

cards.title: Agencies and DMCs
cards.body: Create owned hotel commerce paths for clients, groups, and campaigns.

items.question: Does Wink have APIs?
items.answer: Yes. Wink developer access is planned around hotel search, booking, payment, webhook, reporting, and SDK workflows.

items.question: Who is the Developers page for?
items.answer: It is for platforms, agencies, banks, loyalty programs, super apps, marketplaces, and product teams that want hotel commerce inside their own product.

items.question: Can a platform use Wink without becoming an OTA?
items.answer: Yes. Wink is designed as a hotel commerce layer. Hotels keep control of supply and the guest relationship while platforms build booking paths around approved supply.

items.question: Where should technical users go next?
items.answer: Technical users should start with documentation. Commercial or enterprise users should request a demo.

fields.heading: Ready to build with Wink?
fields.body: Start with the route that matches your team, then move into a focused demo or sign-up flow.
